steps.title: Establish arc relationships from the diameters
steps.content:
Since $PR$ is a diameter, the arc from $P$ to $R$ (passing through $Q$) spans a semicircle:
$$PQ + QR = 72\pi$$ (half of the $144\pi$ circumference). Similarly, since $QS$ is a diameter:
$$QR + RS = 72\pi$$
Subtracting the first equation from the second: $RS = PQ$. Let $PQ = x$. Then $QR = 72\pi - x$ and $RS = x$.

steps.title: Apply the constraint arc PS = 2 × arc PQ
steps.content:
From the arc pattern: $PQ = x$, $QR = 72\pi - x$, $RS = x$, $SP = 72\pi - x$. The shorter arc $PS = 72\pi - x$. Given $PS = 2 \cdot PQ$:
$$72\pi - x = 2x$$
$$72\pi = 3x$$
$$x = 24\pi$$

steps.title: Calculate arc QR and identify the answer
steps.content:
$QR = 72\pi - x = 72\pi - 24\pi = 48\pi$.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
